---

# MSBuild Project Review: ContosoApp Solution

## Summary

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Projects | 5 (3 src, 2 test) |
| SDK Style | ✅ All projects use SDK-style format |
| Target Framework | net8.0 (all) |
| Directory.Build.props | ❌ Missing |
| Directory.Build.targets | ❌ Missing |
| Central Package Management | ❌ Not used |
| global.json | ❌ Missing |

---

## 🔴 Errors (Likely to cause issues)

### 🔴 E1: Package version drift — `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions`

The same package is referenced at **two different versions** across the solution:

| Project | Version |
|---|---|
| `ContosoApp.Core` | **8.0.0** |
| `ContosoApp.Services` | **8.0.1** |

Since `ContosoApp.Api` references both `Core` and `Services`, NuGet will silently resolve to 8.0.1 at runtime, but `Core` was compiled and tested against 8.0.0. This creates a subtle inconsistency and — in more complex dependency graphs — can surface as **NU1605 (package downgrade)** warnings or runtime binding failures.

**Fix:** Centralize versions via Central Package Management (see Suggestion S1 below), or immediately align both to `8.0.1`.

---

### 🔴 E2: Test framework version skew between test projects

The two test projects use **different versions** of core test infrastructure packages:

| Package | `Api.Tests` | `Core.Tests` |
|---|---|---|
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | 17.9.0 | **17.10.0** |
| `xunit` | 2.7.1 | **2.8.1** |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | 2.5.6 | **2.8.1** |

Running tests across the solution uses two different test runners simultaneously. This can lead to confusing test discovery or execution failures when behavior differs between versions.

**Fix:** Align to the latest version in all test projects (or, better, adopt Central Package Management).

---

## 🟡 Warnings (Anti-patterns that should be fixed)

### 🟡 W1: Massive property duplication across all 5 projects

The following **8 properties** are copy-pasted identically in every single `.csproj`:

```xml
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
<LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
<Nullable>enable</Nullable>
<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
<Company>Contoso</Company>
<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

This is the textbook case for a `Directory.Build.props` file. Every time you add a project or change a policy (e.g., upgrading to `net9.0`), you must touch all 5 files — a guaranteed source of drift.

**Fix:** See Suggestion S2 below.

---

### 🟡 W2: Duplicated `DocumentationFile` and `PrintBuildInfo` target in all 3 src projects

All three source projects contain identical blocks:

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
</PropertyGroup>

<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
</Target>
```

These are duplicated across `ContosoApp.Api`, `ContosoApp.Core`, and `ContosoApp.Services`.

**Fix:** Move both to a `Directory.Build.targets` file (targets belong in `.targets`, not `.props`, because they execute after project evaluation). Conditionally scope them to `src/` projects only — see Suggestion S3.

---

### 🟡 W3: `LangVersion=latest` restates the SDK default

For .NET 8 SDK, `LangVersion` already defaults to the latest supported C# version for the target framework. Setting it to `latest` is redundant and can mask intent — `latest` may pick up preview language features, whereas the SDK default (`12` for net8.0) is stable.

**Fix:** Either remove `LangVersion` entirely (to use the SDK default), or set it to an explicit version like `12` if you want deterministic behavior. If you intentionally want preview features, keep `latest` but add a comment explaining why.

---

### 🟡 W4: Two competing JSON serialization libraries

| Project | Library |
|---|---|
| `ContosoApp.Core` | `System.Text.Json` 8.0.5 |
| `ContosoApp.Services` | `Newtonsoft.Json` 13.0.3 |

Both are pulled into `ContosoApp.Api` transitively. Having two JSON serializers in the same application increases binary size, creates serialization behavior inconsistencies (different default casing, null handling, etc.), and confuses developers about which to use.

**Fix:** Standardize on `System.Text.Json` (the modern, built-in option) unless `Newtonsoft.Json` is required for specific features (e.g., `JsonPath`, `JObject` manipulation, or third-party SDK requirements). If both are truly needed, document the reason clearly.

---

### 🟡 W5: `xunit.runner.visualstudio` missing `PrivateAssets="all"`

In both test projects:
```xml
<PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="..." />
```

This is a development-time-only test adapter. Without `PrivateAssets="all"`, it will flow transitively if these test projects are ever referenced by another project (unlikely but still a hygiene issue).

**Fix:**
```xml
<PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="..." PrivateAssets="all" />
```

Similarly, `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` should also have `PrivateAssets="all"` for the same reason.

---

### 🟡 W6: No `global.json` for SDK version pinning

Without a `global.json`, each developer and CI agent uses whatever .NET SDK version is installed, which can produce different build results. This is especially important when multiple SDK versions are installed side by side.

**Fix:** Add a root-level `global.json`:
```json
{
  "sdk": {
    "version": "8.0.400",
    "rollForward": "latestPatch"
  }
}
```

---

## 🔵 Suggestions (Improvements)

### 🔵 S1: Adopt NuGet Central Package Management (CPM)

Create a `Directory.Packages.props` at the solution root to centralize all package versions. This **eliminates E1 and E2** by design.

**Proposed `Directory.Packages.props`:**
```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Shared -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />

    <!-- Web -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />

    <!-- Testing -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

Then each `.csproj` drops the `Version` attribute:
```xml
<PackageReference Include="xunit" />
```

---

### 🔵 S2: Create `Directory.Build.props` for shared settings

**Proposed `/Directory.Build.props`:**
```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

Additionally, create **`/test/Directory.Build.props`** for test-specific defaults:
```xml
<Project>
  <!-- Import parent first -->
  <Import Project="$([MSBuild]::GetPathOfFileAbove('Directory.Build.props', '$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)../'))" />

  <PropertyGroup>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" PrivateAssets="all" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" PrivateAssets="all" />
    <PackageReference Include="NSubstitute" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

This reduces test `.csproj` files to just their unique `ProjectReference`.

---

### 🔵 S3: Create `Directory.Build.targets` for shared targets

**Proposed `/src/Directory.Build.targets`:**
```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>
</Project>
```

Note: `DocumentationFile` must go in `.targets` (not `.props`) because `$(OutputPath)` isn't fully resolved during the `.props` evaluation phase. The current approach of setting it in the `.csproj` works but centralizing it in `.targets` is correct and DRY.

---

### 🔵 S4: Resulting simplified project files

After applying S1 + S2 + S3, the project files shrink dramatically:

**`ContosoApp.Core.csproj`** (from 28 lines → 10):
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
```

**`ContosoApp.Api.csproj`** (from 33 lines → 14):
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web">

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
    <ProjectReference Include="..\ContosoApp.Services\ContosoApp.Services.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
```

**`ContosoApp.Core.Tests.csproj`** (from 26 lines → 8):
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\..\src\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
```

---

## 🟢 Positive (Done well)

| Practice | Assessment |
|---|---|
| ✅ SDK-style projects | All 5 projects use the modern SDK-style format |
| ✅ Nullable reference types | Enabled consistently across all projects |
| ✅ `TreatWarningsAsErrors` | Enforced in all projects — excellent quality gate |
| ✅ `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` | Consistent code style enforcement |
| ✅ `IsPackable=false` on test projects | Prevents accidentally publishing test assemblies |
| ✅ Clean project references | Correct relative paths, no circular dependencies |
| ✅ Consistent SDK (net8.0) | All projects target the same framework |

---

## Summary of Recommended Actions

| Priority | Action | Fixes |
|---|---|---|
| 1️⃣ | Create `Directory.Build.props` at repo root | W1, W3 |
| 2️⃣ | Create `Directory.Packages.props` (CPM) | E1, E2 |
| 3️⃣ | Create `test/Directory.Build.props` for test defaults | W1 (test portion), W5 |
| 4️⃣ | Create `src/Directory.Build.targets` for shared target + docfile | W2 |
| 5️⃣ | Add `global.json` | W6 |
| 6️⃣ | Standardize JSON serializer | W4 |
| 7️⃣ | Strip duplicated properties/packages from all `.csproj` files | Cleanup |

Implementing items 1–4 would reduce the total MSBuild XML across the solution by approximately **60%** and eliminate every class of version-drift bug found in this review.
